name: MaxEisen.me
shortDescription: My personal portfolio website (the one you're currently on) — built from scratch and home to a live photo gallery and ambient dashboard
bullets: A personal portfolio website built from scratch in Svelte to showcase my experience, projects, and skills
Includes a photo gallery backed by Cloudinary with location metadata, lazy-loaded masonry layout, and an arrow-key lightbox
Hosts an ambient dashboard wiring together live Spotify, Strava, GitHub, Hacker News, and weather data via Netlify functions
Continuously iterated on over the years, serving as a living canvas for improving my design and front-end development skills
Deployed via Netlify with automatic builds on every push to the main branch

name: Convoke
shortDescription: A web-based game designed to provoke abstract conversation and deep thought
bullets: A social party game built to spark abstract conversation, using OpenAI's GPT-3 API to generate thought-provoking questions each round
Features a daily question mode for solo reflection, with responses persisted to local storage
Launched independently in two weeks, reaching #7 on Product Hunt on launch day

name: WhatToTip
shortDescription: A web app to helping travellers save money on their next trip
bullets: A minimalist travel companion app providing money-saving tips for every country, built with Next.js for fast, responsive interactions
Leveraged OpenAI's GPT-3 engine via a custom Python script to generate country-specific tips at scale
Continuously deployed from GitHub via Vercel, with static content served efficiently through server-side rendering

name: NFTokenator
shortDescription: A Python script to programatically generate NFT images from provided assets
bullets: A CLI-based NFT image generator capable of producing tens of thousands of unique tokens in minutes from layered assets
Uses Pillow to composite randomized trait layers according to configurable rarity weights defined by the user
Designed to be easily forked and extended with custom assets, traits, and validation logic for any NFT collection

name: NuHealth
shortDescription: A web app to resolve the bottlenecks in Canada's socialized healthcare system
bullets: A dual-purpose health platform letting patients control access to their medical records while giving EMTs instant access to critical data in the field
Built with Next.js and Firebase, supporting real-time data access and secure authentication for first responders
Developed in under 48 hours by a team of 4 for the Publicis Sapient Aspire SPEED Hackathon, placing as a top 10 finalist

name: Eagle
shortDescription: A delivery service comparison platform to help food-lovers get the best deal
bullets: A React Native mobile app that aggregates pricing, delivery times, and reviews for the same restaurant across four major delivery platforms
Ideated, built, and shipped by a team of 10 under my direction as product manager within QTMA
Designed to help users make informed delivery choices, cutting through fragmented platforms with a single unified view

name: Studii
shortDescription: A collaborative, all-in-one study space made for students, by students
bullets: A real-time peer study platform connecting students who struggle to find effective study methods or compatible study partners
Built with React, Django, and MongoDB by a team of 8, handling live collaboration across courses and subjects
Conceived, developed, marketed, and pitched end-to-end as part of the QTMA product incubator program

name: QHacks
shortDescription: The official website for Queen's University's 2020 MLH hackathon
bullets: The official static website for Queen's University's 2020 MLH-affiliated hackathon, built with React and Gatsby
Served as the primary hub for 700+ applicants and received thousands of visits throughout the application and pre-event period
Supported the full event lifecycle from application through to the day of, keeping hackers informed and engaged in real time

name: Spotilizer
shortDescription: A customizable, data-centric Spotify music visualizer built in Python
bullets: A music visualizer that connects to a user's Spotify account and renders dynamic visuals driven by data from the Spotify Web API
Translates audio features like energy, rhythm, and danceability into real-time visual output using Python and Tkinter
Built by a team of 4 in 10 hours at MLH's 2019 Local Hack Day at Queen's University, winning 2nd place

name: Glitch
shortDescription: A unique, monochromatic platformer game for observant minimalists
bullets: A monochromatic Unity platformer built around a core mechanic where players toggle between two states — glitched and default — to navigate levels
Designed to reward observation and lateral thinking, with puzzles that only resolve when the player finds the right state combination
Developed by a team of 3 as a final project for CISC 226 (Game Design) at Queen's University
